price_text: Starting around $5,500 per WPJA for micro weddings
price_details: WPJA lists her starting around $5,500. Site is built around Colorado Micro Wedding Collections (30-page and 20-page heirloom album tiers), with albums, engagement add-ons, and rehearsal coverage bolted on separately rather than bundled by default. She sends a full pricing guide only after an initial contact, then does a video call before booking, so there's a screening step before you see real numbers.
notes: Spent a while on Nina's site because she has a whole elopement and micro-wedding angle that's different from most photographers we looked at. She's explicitly LGBTQ+ inclusive with a dedicated page on LGBTQ+ elopements, shoots a film and digital hybrid, and has apparently done 300+ weddings across the Rocky Mountains per WPJA. Turnaround is fast, sneak peeks within two days and the full gallery within six weeks, which is on the quicker end of what we've seen. One nice detail: no travel fee if your elopement is in Aspen. You do have to go through her contact form and a video call before getting real pricing though, it's not just posted up front.

venue: Photography: Family, Engagement, Wedding, Portrait etc.
price_details: no site to check pricing on at all (google listing only), and none of the three reviews we read mention a number. 15 reviews total at 5 stars though, which is a strong signal even without pricing
notes: - Reviewer eloped in Truckee w/ 18 family/friends and said Marta made picking a package easy, asked good questions to get her creative ideas flowing - Another bride said the photos captured the day's vibrancy 'absolutely perfectly' and specifically leaned on Marta for day-of timing advice since they skipped a planner - A separate reviewer hired her for a surprise Father's Day family shoot, said she was great wrangling kids and turned around digital photos within a few days - No site, no pricing anywhere, New Castle based per the listing

price_details: site has actual tiered pricing up front: 4-hour coverage $2,800, 6-hour $3,400, 8-hour $5,200 (the 8-hour is the full-day option, 700+ edited images, online gallery, delivery in 3 weeks). Elopement half-day (up to 4 hrs) is $2,200 and includes a location scouting consult, full day elopement is $3,800. There's also a small wedding/elopement mini session at $1,375 and a 35mm film add-on where pricing depends on film stock cost, inquire direct. Travel fees are case by case depending on where the wedding falls in the year, no flat number given. Non-refundable retainer required to hold the date, standard industry stuff.
notes: Elliot (goes by Henry per the site, a little confusing) documentary-style, described as bold and colorful, been shooting weddings about 10 years. What stood out is the site actually publishes real numbers instead of making you ask, which was rare in our search. He also does custom packages for couples who don't fit a normal mold and specifically says he won't price gouge on travel, for whatever that's worth coming from the vendor themselves. Album design is optional, he'll just point you to a printer if you want to DIY it. No third-party reviews came up in our search so this is mostly straight from his own site.

price_details: Site says wedding collections start at $3,800, with custom collections built for couples whose day doesn't fit a set tier, and payment plans available. No hourly breakdown or add-on list given beyond that starting figure.
notes: Went down her site a bit and the style leans lighter and true to color, not overly warm or moody. Wedding collections start at $3,800 but she also builds custom ones and offers payment plans, which is a nice option if the full amount up front is a stretch. A lot of her portfolio examples are engagement sessions (saw one at Brainard Lake specifically), so if you want a preview of how she shoots before booking the wedding day, there's plenty to look at. No reviews came up in what we checked, so take the site's own framing with a grain of salt.

price_text: Elopements $3,000-$10,500 by tier, single Wedding Collection price not listed on site
price_details: Elopement tiers are spelled out: Mini Local $3,000, Half Day $4,200, Full Day $7,000, Multi-Day $10,500. For actual weddings she only offers one Wedding Collection (no hourly tiers to compare), and the site doesn't publish that number, you'd need to inquire. Add-ons are flat $1,200 each regardless of type, covers an engagement session, a post-wedding hiking session, rehearsal dinner coverage, or an album. $1,300 retainer to book, balance due anytime up to 30 days before the wedding.
notes: This one's interesting because she deliberately does ONE wedding collection instead of hourly tiers, her whole pitch is that hourly limits suck and she doesn't want you rushing your timeline to squeeze in photos. Elopement pricing is fully published though and ranges $3,000 to $10,500 depending on mini/half/full/multi-day. She's Colorado focused with a specific mountain elopement angle, her site literally points out that different parts of the state have different rules around elopement permits, which is a detail I hadn't thought about. Add-ons (engagement session, hiking session, rehearsal dinner, album) are all a flat $1,200 each, can be tacked on anytime, not just at booking.

price_text: Weddings start at $3,750, elopements/micro-weddings by inquiry
price_details: Regular weddings start at $3,750 (coverage length not specified beyond 'starting'), micro-weddings under 35 people are quote-only via the photographer (Piper). Engagement sessions start at $400 (1.5 hrs), portrait sessions start at $375 (30 min). Film add-ons run $50-$125 depending on how many rolls of 35mm you want processed and scanned, more available on request. Travel fees are shoot-location dependent, no flat rate given. Rebooking fee is $500 if you reschedule a June-August weekend wedding; deposits are non-refundable on cancellation for both weddings and portraits.
notes: Her editorial style comes through even in the pricing page copy, which reads more personal than most (she's been to 50 countries and clearly loves travel shoots). Weddings start at $3,750 and portraits/engagements are priced separately and lower, $400 and $375 respectively. The film add-on menu is a nice touch if you want some 35mm mixed into a digital package, $50 for one roll up to $125 for three. One wart: if you need to reschedule a summer weekend wedding there's a $500 rebooking fee, and deposits are non-refundable across the board on cancellation, so read the contract terms closely before signing.

price_text: $2,500-$9,000 range depending on resort area, 7-hour package $3,450
price_details: Site breaks pricing out by mountain town (Beaver Creek, Breckenridge, Keystone, Steamboat, Vail) with the same general range quoted for each: $2,500-$9,000. The one hard package number given is a 7-hour wedding photography package at $3,450, which includes fully edited images delivered within 72 hours, ready to print. No separate line items for engagement sessions or second shooter found.
notes: Tom shoots documentary style and specifically markets himself around the mountain resort towns, Vail, Beaver Creek, Breckenridge, Keystone, Steamboat all get their own pricing page but the range quoted is the same $2,500 to $9,000 across the board. The one concrete package is 7 hours for $3,450 with a genuinely fast turnaround, 72 hours for fully edited images, which is quicker than most photographers we've looked at. A Fort Collins bride's review mentioned he was reasonably priced, got to know the couple a bit before the wedding, and complimented his angles, so that tracks with the site's own positioning.
service_region: Mountain resort towns (Vail, Beaver Creek, Breckenridge, Keystone, Steamboat) RICH:tom-k-photo

price_details: Bronze (6 hrs) $2,600, Silver (8 hrs) $3,000, Gold (10 hrs) $3,400, Diamond (10 hrs lead + 5 hrs second photographer) $5,000. Second photographer can also be added a la carte at $150/hr to any tier. 1.5-hour engagement session is a separate $600 add-on. A regional digest lists starting rates as low as $2,100-$2,500 ($2,500 specifically for the Boulder area), so the published site tiers above may reflect a later price update.
notes: Their pricing page is genuinely one of the more transparent ones we found, full tiered breakdown from Bronze up through Diamond with hours and second-shooter inclusion spelled out for each. Style is photojournalistic with warm, true-to-life editing per their own framing, and a digest we found says they've shot 500+ weddings and deliver around 1,300 final edited images with a private gallery (50 sneak peeks within 3 weeks of the wedding). Based in Denver/Broomfield. Only wart is the digest quotes a lower starting price than what's on the live pricing page now, so treat the $2,600 Bronze tier as the more current number and double check before assuming the cheaper figure still applies.
